name: Aldon Smith
title: Last known photo of Aldon Smith, taken the morning of June 14, 2026, after he dropped off pizzas for a homeless outreach in San Jose, about an hour before his death.
description: This is the last known photograph of Aldon Smith, from a series taken on the morning of Saturday, June 14, 2026, beside a homeless encampment in San Jose, California. Wearing a Willis Reed throwback New York Knicks jersey, Smith had made an unplanned stop with his friend Amir Shirazi to drop off ten Little Caesars pizzas for the homeless. Pastor Scott Wagers, co-founder of CHAM Deliverance Ministry, a San Jose outreach that has fed people in the city's encampments since 1997, met them there. Wagers, who had never met Smith, later said he looked tired and lethargic. After the stop, Smith and Shirazi drove to a grocery store and then to Shirazi's home in Los Gatos. While Shirazi briefly stepped inside, he came back out to find Smith slumped over and unresponsive in the truck. Smith was pronounced dead that afternoon at 12:46 p.m. He was 36 years old.
bio: Aldon Smith was an American football defensive end who played six seasons in the NFL. The seventh overall pick of the San Francisco 49ers in 2011, he recorded 33.5 sacks in his first two seasons, including a franchise single-season record of 19.5 in 2012, and helped the 49ers reach Super Bowl XLVII. He also played for the Oakland Raiders and Dallas Cowboys. Off the field, he faced a long battle with addiction that he spoke about openly and worked hard to overcome.
additionalImagesNote: Plate 02: Smith (left) with Pastor Scott Wagers (center) and his friend Amir Shirazi (right) at the tailgate during the drop-off. Plate 03: Smith lifting a box from the stack of pizzas in the truck bed.
